volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Persians (Section 759 - Section 2.965)

sections.label: Section 759
sections.translation: Therefore a calamity

sections.label: Section 760
sections.translation: dreadful and unforgettable has been caused by him , a desolating calamity such as never before befell this city of Susa since our Lord Zeus first ordained that one ruler should bear sway over all Asia with its flocks and wield the sceptre of its government .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: Μῆδος γὰρ ἦν [*] ὁ πρῶτος ἡγεμὼν στρατοῦ :
sections.translation: For Medus was first to be the leader of its host ; and another , his son , completed his work since wisdom ruled his spirit . Third , after him , Cyrus , blessed in good fortune , came to the throne and established peace for all his people .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: Λυδῶν δὲ λαὸν καὶ Φρυγῶν ἐκτήσατο ,
sections.translation: The Lydians and Phrygians he won to his rule , and the whole of Ionia he subdued by force ; for he won the favor of the gods through his right-mindedness . Fourth in succession , the son of Cyrus ruled the host . Fifth in the list , Mardus came to power , a disgrace to his native land

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: θρόνοισί τ ἀρχαίοισι : τὸν δὲ σὺν δόλῳ
sections.translation: and to the ancient throne ; but he was slain in his palace by the guile of noble Artaphrenes , with the help of friends whose duty this was . Sixth came Maraphis , and seventh Artaphrenes . [*] And I in turn attained the lot I desired ,

sections.label: Section 780
sections.translation: and many a campaign I made with a valiant force ; but disaster so dire as this I brought not upon the state . Yet Xerxes my son , youth that he is , has the mind of youth and does not remember my injunctions . Be sure of this , my age-mates :

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ἅπαντες ἡμεῖς , οἳ κράτη τάδ ἔσχομεν ,
sections.translation: all of us who have held this sovereign power cannot be shown to have wrought ruin as great as this .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: τί οὖν , ἄναξ Δαρεῖε , ποῖ καταστρέφεις
sections.translation: What then , O king Darius ? What is the intention of your words ? How , after this reverse , may we , the people of Persia , best prosper in time to come ?

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: εἰ μὴ στρατεύοισθ ἐς τὸν Ἑλλήνων τόπον ,
sections.translation: If you do not take the field against the Hellenes ’ land , even if the forces of the Medes outnumber theirs . The land itself is their ally .

sections.label: Section 793
sections.original: πῶς τοῦτ ἔλεξας , τίνι τρόπῳ δὲ συμμαχεῖ ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? In what way their ally ?

sections.label: Section 794
sections.translation: It wastes with famine an enemy force which is too large .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ἀλλ εὐσταλῆ [*] τοι λεκτὸν ἀροῦμεν στόλον .
sections.translation: But we will dispatch a force of select and easily managed troops .

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: ἀλλ οὐδ ὁ μείνας νῦν ἐν Ἑλλάδος τόποις [*]
sections.translation: Not even the host which now remains in Hellas will be able to return to safety .

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; οὐ γὰρ πᾶν στράτευμα βαρβάρων
sections.translation: How is that ? Will not the whole barbarian army cross from Europe over the Hellespont ?

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: παῦροί γε πολλῶν , εἴ τι πιστεῦσαι θεῶν
sections.translation: Few indeed out of many , if it is right to put any faith in the oracles of Heaven , with regard to what has just been brought to pass ; for they are fulfilled , not just some , while others fail . And if this is truth , it is through persuasion of vain hopes that he is leaving behind a body of picked troops .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: μίμνουσι δ ἔνθα πεδίον Ἀσωπὸς ῥοαῖς
sections.translation: They are now lingering where the plain is watered by the stream of Asopus which nourishes Boeotia ’ s fields . Here they will meet their crowning disaster in requital for their presumptuous pride and impious thoughts . For , on reaching the land of Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 810
sections.translation: restrained by no religious awe , they ravaged the images of the gods and set fire to their temples . Altars have been destroyed , statues of the gods have been thrown from their bases in utter ruin and confusion . Therefore , since they wrought such evil , evil they suffer in no less measure ; and other evils are still in store :

sections.label: Section 815
sections.translation: the spring of their woes is not yet quenched , but it still wells forth . For so great will be the mass of clotted gore spilled by the Dorian lance upon Plataean soil that heaps of dead will reveal , even to the third generation , a voiceless record for the eyes of men

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: ὡς οὐχ ὑπέρφευ θνητὸν ὄντα χρὴ φρονεῖν .
sections.translation:
that mortal man should not vaunt himself excessively . For presumptuous pride , when it has matured , bears as its fruit a crop of calamity , from which it reaps an abundant harvest of tears .
Bear in mind that such are the penalties for deeds like these , and hold Athens and Hellas in your memory . Let no one of you ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.translation: through disdain of present fortune and lust for more , squander his abundant wealth . Zeus , in truth , is a chastiser of overweening pride and corrects with heavy hand . Therefore , now that my son has been warned to be prudent by the voice of God ,

sections.label: Section 830
sections.translation: instruct him with admonitions of reason to cease from drawing the punishment of Heaven on himself by his vaunting rashness . And as for you , beloved and venerable mother of Xerxes , withdraw to the palace and bring from there clothing which is suitable for him , and prepare to meet your son . For

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: κακῶν ὑπ ἄλγους λακίδες ἀμφὶ σώματι
sections.translation: through grief at his misfortunes , the embroidered apparel which he was wearing has been torn into tattered shreds . Soothe him with words of kindness ; for it is to your voice alone , I know , that he will listen . As for me , I depart to the darkness beneath the earth .

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δέ , πρέσβεις , χαίρετ , ἐν κακοῖς ὅμως
sections.translation: Farewell , Elders , and despite your troubles , rejoice while each day is yours ; for wealth does not profit the dead at all . The ghost of Darius descends

sections.label: Section 843
sections.original: ἦ πολλὰ καὶ παρόντα καὶ μέλλοντ ἔτι
sections.translation: I grieve as I hear the many misfortunes that are now , and are yet to be , the lot of the barbarians .

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ὦ δαῖμον , ὥς με πόλλ ἐσέρχεται [*] κακῶν [*]
sections.translation: O God ! How much grief assails me ! But most of all this sorrow wounds me , to hear of the shameful clothes which are now worn by my son . But I will depart , and when I have brought appropriate garments from the palace ,

sections.label: Section 850
sections.translation: I will make attempt to meet my son ; for I will not forsake him whom I love so well in his affliction . Exit

sections.label: Section 1.852
sections.original: ὦ πόποι ἦ μεγάλας ἀγαθᾶς τε πο-
sections.translation: Oh yes , it was in truth a glorious and good life under civil government that we enjoyed so long as our aged

sections.label: Section 1.855
sections.translation: and all-powerful king , who did no wrong and did not favor war , god-like Darius , ruled the realm .

sections.label: Section 1.858
sections.translation: In the first place we showed to the world armies worthy of our fame , and civil institutions , like towers in strength ,

sections.label: Section 1.860
sections.translation: regulated all the state ; and our return from war brought back our men , unworn and unsuffering , to happy homes .

sections.label: Section 2.864
sections.translation: And what a number of cities he captured !—

sections.label: Section 2.865
sections.translation: without crossing the stream of Halys or even stirring from his own hearth : such as the Acheloan [*] cities on the Strymonian sea which is located beside

sections.label: Section 2.870
sections.translation: the Thracian settlements .

sections.label: Section 2.871
sections.translation: And those outside the lake , the cities on the mainland , surrounded with a rampart , obeyed him as their king ;

sections.label: Section 2.875
sections.translation: those , too , that boast to be on both sides of the broad Hellespont and Propontis , deeply-recessed , and the outlet of Pontus .

sections.label: Section 3.880
sections.translation: of the sea , lying close to this land of ours , such as Lesbos , and olive-planted Samos , Chios and Paros , Naxos , Mykonos ,

sections.label: Section 3.885
sections.translation: and Andros which lies adjacent to Tenos .

sections.label: Section 3.888
sections.translation: And he held under his sway the sea-girt islands midway between the continents ,

sections.label: Section 3.890
sections.translation: Lemnos , and the settlement of Icarus , and Rhodes , and Cnidos , and the Cyprian cities Paphos , Soli , and Salamis ,

sections.label: Section 898
sections.translation: And the rich and populous cities of the Hellenes in the Ionian heritage

sections.label: Section 900
sections.translation: he controlled by his own will ; and at his command he had an unwearied strength of men-at-arms and of allies from every nation . But now ,

sections.label: Section 905
sections.translation: worsted completely in war through disasters on the sea , we endure this change of fortune no doubt from the hand of god .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.translation: Alas , wretched am I who have met this cruel doom

sections.label: Section 910
sections.translation: which did not give the faintest sign of its coming ! In what savage mood has Fortune trampled upon the Persian race ? What misery is yet in store for me , unhappy wretch ? The strength of my limbs is loosened as I look upon this aged group of citizens .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: εἴθ ὄφελεν , Ζεῦ , κἀμὲ μετ ἀνδρῶν
sections.translation: Ah , Zeus , I wish that the doom of death had buried me , too , together with the men who have been laid low !

sections.label: Section 918
sections.translation: Alas , my king , for our noble army , for the high honor of Persia ’ s rule ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.translation: and for the splendor of the men now cut off by Fate ! The land bewails her native youth , slaughtered for Xerxes , who has crowded Hades with Persian slain .

sections.label: Section 1.925
sections.translation: Many warriors , masters of the bow , our country ’ s pride , a great multitude of men , have perished . Alas , alas , for our trusty defence ! The land of Asia , the leading power of the earth ,

sections.label: Section 1.930
sections.translation: has piteously , yes piteously , been bowed to her knees .

sections.label: Section 1.932
sections.translation: Behold me , alas , a fitting subject for lamentation , born a source of ruin to my race and fatherland .

sections.label: Section 1.935
sections.translation: As a greeting for your return home I will lift up an ill-omened cry of woe , the voice , versed in lamentation , of a Mariandynian mourner ,

sections.label: Section 1.940
sections.translation: a wail attended by many a tear .

sections.label: Section 1.941
sections.translation: Utter a strain of lamentation , plaintive and discordant . For Fortune has now veered and turned against me .

sections.label: Section 1.944
sections.translation: I will indeed utter the song of lamentation

sections.label: Section 1.945
sections.translation: in commemoration of your sufferings and of our strongly-manned ships , buffeted by waves ; the plaintive strain of our land which mourns its sons . And I will cry aloud in lamentation , shedding many a tear .

sections.label: Section 2.950
sections.translation: Yes , for the Ionian naval force , turning the tide of battle , swept them away , the Ionian host , ravaging the dark sea and the shore of doom .

sections.label: Section 2.955
sections.translation: Woe ! woe ! cry aloud , learn about the whole disaster . Where is the rest of the multitude of your comrades ? Where are those who stood by your side , such as Pharandaces , Susas , Pelagon , Dotamas , and

sections.label: Section 2.960
sections.translation: Agdabatas , Psammis , and Susiscanes of Agbatana ?

sections.label: Section 2.962
sections.translation: By the shores of Salamis , dashing against its rugged shore ,

sections.label: Section 2.965
sections.translation: I left them , fallen in death from a Tyrian ship .
